top_stories.title: Microsoft 365 users targeted by new phishing threat that bypasses MFA
top_stories.summary: The FBI has issued a warning about Kali365, a Phishing-as-a-Service platform first observed in April 2026 that captures Microsoft 365 OAuth access tokens to bypass MFA without stealing user credentials. The platform is distributed via Telegram and includes AI-generated phishing lures, automated campaign templates, real-time tracking dashboards, and OAuth token capture capabilities.

top_stories.title: The New Phishing Click: How OAuth Consent Bypasses MFA
top_stories.summary: A phishing-as-a-service platform called EvilTokens launched in February 2026 and compromised over 340 Microsoft 365 organizations across five countries within five weeks. The attack method uses OAuth consent flows and device code phishing to bypass MFA, tricking users into authenticating at a legitimate Microsoft URL while handing over OAuth tokens to attackers.

top_stories.title: Announcing The Forrester Wave™: Workforce Identity Platforms, Q2 2026
top_stories.summary: Forrester has released its Q2 2026 Wave evaluation covering workforce identity security platforms. The report assesses and ranks major vendors in the workforce identity security market, which encompasses authentication, SSO, MFA, and related IAM capabilities.

top_stories.title: Microsoft Self-Service Password Reset abused in Azure data theft attacks
top_stories.summary: A threat actor is actively abusing Microsoft's Self-Service Password Reset (SSPR) feature alongside legitimate applications to steal data from Microsoft 365 and Azure production environments. The attacks exploit native IAM administration features, making them difficult to detect through standard security monitoring.

top_stories.title: AI Agents Are Shifting Identity Security Budget Dynamics
top_stories.summary: New Omdia research examines how AI agent identity management is creating distinct budget dynamics compared to traditional IAM projects. The research highlights that AI agent identities require dedicated management, security, and governance approaches as AI agent deployments proliferate across enterprises.

news.title: Deleted Google API keys keep working for up to 23 minutes, researchers warn
news.summary: Aikido Security researchers discovered that deleted Google API keys remain functional for up to 23 minutes after deletion. This affects credential revocation reliability, as leaked keys used for Google services including Gemini AI can still authenticate and access data during this window despite being deleted.

news.title: Versa extends zero trust principles to AI agents and MCP workflows
news.summary: Versa has introduced a patent-pending zero trust architecture for the Model Context Protocol (MCP), applying identity-based access controls and role-based policies to AI agent actions before execution. The system validates every AI-generated action against user identity, RBAC, and system policies, with optional human approval gates configured by administrators.

news.title: Proton Pass adds monitored credential sharing for AI agents
news.summary: Proton Pass has added AI access tokens that enable monitored credential sharing with AI agents, requiring agents to provide a reason for access requests. The feature allows users to grant AI agents access to selected password vault items while monitoring their activity, and is available across paid Proton Pass plans.

news.title: Lawmakers Demand Answers as CISA Tries to Contain Data Leak
news.summary: A CISA contractor intentionally published AWS GovCloud keys and other agency secrets to a public GitHub account, resulting in a data breach that CISA is still working to contain. Lawmakers from both chambers of Congress are demanding answers while CISA works to invalidate the leaked credentials.

news.title: OktaがClaude Compliance APIと統合、Claude環境内のアイデンティティ保護を強化
news.summary: Okta has integrated its Identity Security Posture Management (ISPM) product with Anthropic's Claude Compliance API to strengthen identity protection within Claude environments. The integration extends Okta's ISPM capabilities to monitor and secure identities operating within Claude-based AI environments.

news.title: Microsoft Warns of Two Actively Exploited Defender Vulnerabilities
news.summary: Microsoft disclosed two actively exploited vulnerabilities in Microsoft Defender, including a privilege escalation flaw (CVE-2026-41091, CVSS 7.8) that could allow attackers to gain SYSTEM privileges. The second vulnerability involves a denial-of-service flaw, with both confirmed as under active exploitation in the wild.

news.title: When Identity is the Attack Path
news.summary: An analysis examines how a single cached AWS access key on a Windows machine created an attack path to approximately 98% of a company's cloud entities. The scenario illustrates how standard credential caching behavior, without any misconfiguration or policy violation, can result in critical identity-based cloud exposure.

news.title: Why Okta (OKTA) Sees AI Agents as an Identity-Security Opportunity
news.summary: Okta is positioning AI agents as an identity security opportunity, framing the technology as a growth area for the company. The article appears primarily focused on OKTA stock performance and investment thesis rather than specific IAM product or policy developments.

news.title: SailPoint Announces New Integration with the Claude Compliance API to Provide Enterprise-Grade Identity Security for AI Platforms
news.summary: SailPoint has announced a new integration with Anthropic's Claude Compliance API, enabling governance and visibility into Claude Enterprise access and usage. The connector extends SailPoint's identity governance capabilities to AI platform access management.

news.title: WSO2 Accelerates Agentic Enterprise Adoption with New Agent Identity, Forward Deployed Engineers, and Expanded Delivery Partner Ecosystem
news.summary: WSO2 has expanded its Agent Fabric platform with new agent identity capabilities targeting AI-native enterprise environments. The update includes AI agent identity management features alongside forward deployed engineering services and an expanded delivery partner ecosystem.

news.title: Semperis Takes Center Stage at Infosec Europe 2026
news.summary: Semperis, an identity-focused cybersecurity vendor, is participating in Infosec Europe 2026 with a panel discussion featuring several security professionals. The panel centers on a presentation titled "Midnight in the War Room" featuring Simon Hodgkinson, Grace Cassy, Sarah Armstrong-Smith, and Jesse McGraw.

news.title: Protecting collaboration with behavior-based access controls
news.summary: The article discusses behavior-based access controls as a security approach for protecting modern collaboration sessions. It positions this method as an evolution beyond traditional access control tools in the context of securing collaborative workflows.

news.title: Implement SAML SSO Authentication in Laravel Filament with Socialite
news.summary: A developer tutorial explains how to implement SAML SSO authentication in a Laravel Filament application using Socialite and a SAML2 provider. The guide covers building authentication flows with metadata endpoints, redirects, and callbacks for enterprise integration.

news.title: Agent AI is Coming. Are You Ready?
news.summary: Orchid Security released its "Identity Gap: Snapshot 2026" report on May 19th, 2026, examining unmanaged identity elements in enterprise environments. The report found that "identity dark matter" — unseen and unmanaged identity components — now accounts for 57% of enterprise identity elements, compared to 43% for visible, managed ones, coinciding with increased enterprise adoption of Agent AI.

news.title: Security Hub Extended expands to 21 curated partner solutions across 9 categories
news.summary: AWS expanded its Security Hub Extended plan to include 21 curated partner solutions across 9 security categories. CyberArk has been added as the identity security partner within this updated offering.

news.title: Provision IAM Announces Strategic Investments from Georgia's Own and InTouch Credit Unions
news.summary: Provision IAM announced strategic investments from Georgia's Own Credit Union and InTouch Credit Union, expanding its Customer Advisory Board. The partnerships represent financial backing and industry alignment for the IAM vendor within the credit union sector.

news.title: Okta (OKTA) Could Raise Its Fiscal 2027 Guidance Amid Improved Demand And Execution, Says Barclays
news.summary: Barclays analysts suggested Okta could raise its fiscal 2027 guidance based on improved demand and execution. The commentary came as part of a broader cloud stock analysis, noting Okta's short float of 5.03% and a cited upside potential of 21.50%.

news.title: Keeper Security Launches ServiceNow Workflow Integration for Governed, Self-Service Vault Management
news.summary: Keeper Security has launched a new integration with ServiceNow that brings vault operations directly into ServiceNow's Service Catalog and Flow Designer. The integration covers access requests, record storage, and endpoint privilege management approvals within ServiceNow workflows.

news.title: CISA Exposes Secrets, Credentials in 'Private' Repo
news.summary: CISA accidentally exposed secrets and credentials in a GitHub repository named "Private-CISA" that was publicly accessible since November 2025. The leaked repository contained sensitive credentials, representing a credential exposure incident at a major U.S. cybersecurity agency.

news.title: Compromised Nx Console 18.95.0 Targeted VS Code Developers with Credential Stealer
news.summary: A compromised version of the Nx Console VS Code extension (version 18.95.0) was published to the Microsoft Visual Studio Code Marketplace, containing credential-stealing malware. The malicious extension had access to over 2.2 million installations across VS Code, Cursor, and JetBrains editors.

news.title: Popular GitHub Action Tags Redirected to Imposter Commit to Steal CI/CD Credentials
news.summary: Threat actors compromised the GitHub Actions workflow "actions-cool/issues-helper" by redirecting all existing repository tags to a malicious imposter commit. The attack is designed to harvest and exfiltrate sensitive credentials, including CI/CD pipeline secrets, to an attacker-controlled server.

news.title: CIRT insights: How to help prevent unauthorized account removals from AWS Organizations
news.summary: The AWS Customer Incident Response Team published insights on tactics used by threat actors to perform unauthorized account removals from AWS Organizations. The article covers preventive controls and configuration guidance relevant to cloud identity governance and access management in AWS environments.

news.title: Azure Files Entra-Only identities: Advancing cloud-native identity and security
news.summary: Microsoft has announced the general availability of Entra-Only identities for Azure Files SMB, enabling identity-based access to SMB file shares using cloud-native Microsoft Entra ID authentication. This eliminates the previous requirement for on-premises Active Directory or hybrid identity configurations when accessing Azure Files over SMB.

news.title: EMA Research Finds Legacy IAM Systems Are Slowing AI Adoption in Financial Sector
news.summary: EMA Research, commissioned by Ory Corp., surveyed 271 IT and security professionals and found that legacy IAM systems are hindering AI adoption in the financial and insurance sectors. Financial institutions show lower full-scale AI production deployment rates (29.7%) compared to the broader market (40.6%), with IAM infrastructure identified as a key bottleneck.

news.title: CISA Admin Leaked AWS GovCloud Keys on Github
news.summary: A CISA contractor publicly exposed credentials for multiple highly privileged AWS GovCloud accounts and numerous internal CISA systems via a public GitHub repository. The leaked files included details on how CISA builds, tests, and deploys software internally, representing a significant government credentials exposure incident.

news.title: ‘Scattered Spider’ Member ‘Tylerb’ Pleads Guilty
news.summary: Tyler Robert Buchanan, a British national and senior member of the Scattered Spider cybercrime group, pleaded guilty to wire fraud conspiracy and aggravated identity theft. The group conducted SMS phishing attacks in 2022 that compromised at least a dozen major technology companies, bypassing authentication controls to steal tens of millions in cryptocurrency.

news.title: Russia Hacked Routers to Steal Microsoft Office Tokens
news.summary: Russian military intelligence hackers are exploiting vulnerabilities in older internet routers to harvest Microsoft Office authentication tokens across more than 18,000 networks. The campaign operates without deploying malware, relying solely on token theft to gain unauthorized access to user accounts.
